briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — steady winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and stable conditions.

Johnston: Use a 3/8 to 3/4-ounce football jig in natural coloring like green pumpkin and bounce it off the bottom for cold-water largemouth that are sitting on or close to the bottom.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a 3/8 to 3/4-ounce football jig in natural coloring like green pumpkin and bounce it off the bottom for cold-water largemouth that are sitting on or close to the bottom.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on rock, riprap, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: In cold-water months, slow drag jigs. Use drift paddles to dial down drift speed and control your presentation for more effective casting and a better hookup ratio. Be methodical to stay in
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on rock, riprap, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: When fishing a football jig on deep rock, make a long cast, let it hit bottom, and drag it slowly — most bites come when the bait pops off a rock as bass follow it on the drag

KVD: On lowland reservoirs with grass (Guntersville, Toledo Bend, Okeechobee), bass don't leave shallow areas even in extreme cold. They stage in slightly deeper areas adjacent to spawning flats — drains, ditches, depressions, holes in the grass. ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable winter conditions position bass at 15-35ft on main lake structure — bluffs, steep points, channel bends. Fish are lethargic and grouped tight. Vertical presentations directly on structure are essential.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
